title: Coleus erici-rosenii
taxonomy: Coleus erici-rosenii is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is commonly known as the 'Electric Blue' or 'Electric Blue Sage'. This plant is native to tropical regions and is known for its vibrant blue flowers. Coleus erici-rosenii is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and indoor spaces due to its attractive foliage and colorful blooms. The classification of this plant is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of the Latin words for its discoverers.
uses: Coleus erici-rosenii is primarily known for its ornamental purposes, but it has also been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties may contribute to its potential use in treating various health conditions, such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. More studies are required to confirm the effectiveness of Coleus erici-rosenii in treating specific health conditions.
benefits: Coleus erici-rosenii is known for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems. Research suggests that Coleus erici-rosenii may also have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential uses.
properties:
Coleus erici-rosenii has been known to possess various therapeutic properties. 
Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could contribute to wound healing and tissue protection. 
The plant has also been investigated for its antimicrobial properties, which may help combat bacterial and fungal infections. 
Additionally, Coleus erici-rosenii has been found to possess immunomodulatory properties, which could aid in regulating the immune system.

constituents: Coleus erici-rosenii contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal and therapeutic properties. Research has identified several specific compounds, such as eriocalyxin C, which is a sesquiterpene lactone, and eriocalyxin B, a sesquiterpene. The exact composition and effects of these compounds are still being studied and researched. Further investigation is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their benefits.
preparations: Coleus erici-rosenii is a plant species, but information on herbal preparations made from it is limited. side_effects: Coleus erici-rosenii is a type of flowering plant, and there is limited information available on its potential side effects when ingested or used as a herbal remedy. intro: Coleus erici-rosenii, also known as African Blue Basil or Blue Plectranthus, is a flowering plant species in the Lamiaceae family, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headaches, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coleus erici-rosenii include rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and caffeic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Coleus erici-rosenii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
